mechanism_narrative: With FEDFUNDS pinned near zero (0.07%), the near-zero cost of capital persistently supports equity valuations by compressing the discount rate applied to forward cash flows, producing a structural positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as long as accommodative policy persists.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d SPX return falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or SPX 20d returns are not significantly positive (t-test p>0.05) while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month acceleration in PAYEMS above a recovery threshold signals strengthening labor demand and consumer income growth, which forward-predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings expectations are revised upward.
failure_condition: In periods where PAYEMS 3-month change exceeds +500k, SPX forward 20d returns are negative or indistinguishable from unconditional baseline (p>0.05) across a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) reflects a joint consumer-and-investment demand surge that only exerts predictive power on equities when both series confirm one another; the interaction produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days that neither series predicts alone.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS YoY growth > 15% AND HOUST level above 1,600k units simultaneously
regime_hint: stimulus-driven consumer and housing demand boom
failure_condition: Joint RSAFS/HOUST strength periods show forward 5d SPX returns with Rank IC below 0.03, or the interaction term's marginal predictive contribution over either single-series model is not statistically significant (p>0.10) in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained uptrend in INDPRO indicates ongoing industrial capacity recovery and normalized supply chains, which supports corporate earnings growth and produces a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d forward returns show no significant positive correlation (Rank IC < 0.03) with INDPRO 3-month trend over any rolling 3-year window, or negative returns occur in over 50% of INDPRO uptrend months.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains near zero, negative real interest rates emerge, initially fueling risk asset rallies via cheap financing, but once inflation surprises exceed market tolerance the same combination triggers fears of premature tightening, reversing the direction of forward returns depending on the inflation acceleration regime.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY inflation exceeds 4% while FEDFUNDS stays below 0.25%
regime_hint: early-cycle inflation acceleration under zero interest rate policy
failure_condition: SPX forward 20d returns remain positive (not negative) in more than 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL YoY exceeds 4% with FEDFUNDS below 0.25%, invalidating the tightening-fear channel.

mechanism_narrative: A discrete drop in UNRATE below a psychologically significant threshold signals confirmation of labor market healing, triggering a short-term risk-on repricing of equities as investors price in reduced slack and stronger consumption over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below 6.0% for the first time in the cycle
failure_condition: SPX 5d forward returns following a UNRATE cross below 6.0% are not significantly positive (p>0.05) across repeated instances, or show negative mean return in more than half of observed crossings.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession recovery regime, positive quarter-over-quarter growth in GDPC1 confirms broad-based economic expansion that sustains investor risk appetite, producing persistent positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days; this relationship is regime-specific and weakens or inverts during late-cycle or contraction regimes.
failure_condition: During periods of positive GDPC1 QoQ growth following a recession, SPX 60d forward returns fail to exceed unconditional baseline returns with statistical significance (p>0.05), or Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising CPIAUCSL combined with a still-dovish FEDFUNDS posture generates a short-lived liquidity-driven rally as investors interpret negative real rates as encouragement to rotate into equities before policy tightening expectations build, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days that fade quickly as the interaction matures.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL MoM increase > 0.5% while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25%
regime_hint: negative real rate liquidity rally phase
failure_condition: SPX 5d forward returns following joint CPIAUCSL MoM > 0.5% and FEDFUNDS < 0.25% show no significant positive mean (p>0.05) or the interaction effect is subsumed entirely by the CPIAUCSL single-series signal in ablation testing.
